--- title: "Fasting, Body Composition, and Lifestyle Patterns by Age Group" format: html --- ## Analysis Objective This report explores how fasting measurements, BMI, body size, sex, and lifestyle-related variables vary across two age groups: - `Age <= 30` - `Age > 30` The objective is to identify descriptive patterns, compare distributions, and examine how anthropometric and metabolic variables behave across these two age-based segments. ## Import necessary libraries ::: {#2c991d7a .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import os import numpy as np import pandas as pd import seaborn as sns from dotenv import load_dotenv ``` ::: ## Set the visual theme ::: {#1a961394 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} sns.set_theme( style="whitegrid", context="notebook", rc={"figure.figsize": (8, 5)} ) ``` ::: ## Load the dataset ::: {#069480e8 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} load_dotenv() dataset_path = os.getenv("CLEANED_DATASET_PATH") if dataset_path is None: raise ValueError("CLEANED_DATASET_PATH is not set in the environment.") df = pd.read_excel(dataset_path) df.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
ID Entry Date Age Sex Address Height (CM) Weight (KG) Fasting ABF Uring Color Fasting ... Diet Height (CM).1 Weight (KG).1 BMI Normalized Address Division District City Corporation Sub-District Thana
0 K-225859 2025-02-25 00:00:00 50.0 F MIRESHARAI CTG NaN NaN 17.0 21.1MMOL ORANGE ... 4 147.0 44.0 20.361886 Mirsharai, Chattogram Chattogram Chattogram NaN Mirsharai Mirsharai Thana
1 K-225401 2025-02-11 00:00:00 35.0 M LOHAGORA CTG NaN NaN 10.0 16.2MMOL GREEN ... 6 160.0 62.0 24.218750 Lohagara, Chattogram Chattogram Chattogram NaN Lohagara Lohagara
2 K-225407 2025-02-11 00:00:00 55.0 M SANDWIP CTG NaN NaN 15.5 22.3MMOL YELLOW ... 6 164.0 61.0 22.679952 Sandwip, Chattogram Chattogram Chattogram NaN Sandwip Sandwip
3 K-225413 2025-02-11 00:00:00 50.0 M ANOWARA CTG NaN NaN 22.7 27.1\nMMOL RED ... 5 151.0 52.0 22.806017 Anwara, Chattogram Chattogram Chattogram NaN Anwara Anwara
4 K-225655 2025-02-19 00:00:00 57.0 M MIRESHARAI CTG NaN NaN 7.6 15.0MMOL NIL ... 5 168.0 76.0 26.927438 Mirsharai, Chattogram Chattogram Chattogram NaN Mirsharai Mirsharai Thana

5 rows × 23 columns

``` ::: ::: ## Initial data overview ### Shape of the dataset ::: {#2b7d70aa .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` (43777, 23) ``` ::: ::: ### Column names ::: {#40b60980 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.columns.tolist() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ``` ['ID', 'Entry Date', 'Age', 'Sex', 'Address', 'Height (CM)', 'Weight (KG)', 'Fasting', 'ABF', 'Uring Color Fasting', 'Uring Color ABF', 'Acitone', 'Albumin', 'Diet', 'Height (CM).1', 'Weight (KG).1', 'BMI', 'Normalized Address', 'Division', 'District', 'City Corporation', 'Sub-District', 'Thana'] ``` ::: ::: ### Check required columns ::: {#5f77a394 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} required_columns = [ "Age", "Sex", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting" ] missing_columns = [col for col in required_columns if col not in df.columns] if missing_columns: raise ValueError(f"Missing required columns: {missing_columns}") else: print("All required columns are present.") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` All required columns are present. ``` ::: ::: ### Data types ::: {#86e1049b .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.dtypes ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=7} ``` ID object Entry Date object Age float64 Sex str Address str Height (CM) float64 Weight (KG) float64 Fasting float64 ABF object Uring Color Fasting object Uring Color ABF object Acitone object Albumin object Diet object Height (CM).1 float64 Weight (KG).1 float64 BMI float64 Normalized Address str Division str District str City Corporation str Sub-District str Thana str dtype: object ``` ::: ::: ### Missing values summary ::: {#5711942c .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} df[required_columns].isnull().sum().sort_values(ascending=False) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=8} ``` Height (CM) 32743 Weight (KG) 32652 BMI 9 Age 0 Sex 0 Fasting 0 dtype: int64 ``` ::: ::: ### Duplicate rows ::: {#f3c4d2c7 .cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.duplicated().sum() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=9} ``` np.int64(0) ``` ::: ::: ## Descriptive statistics ### Numerical summary ::: {#c0790ce6 .cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} df[["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]].describe() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=10} ```{=html}
Age Height (CM) Weight (KG) BMI Fasting
count 43777.000000 11034.000000 11125.000000 43768.000000 43777.000000
mean 47.598006 158.110413 64.855092 41.582140 11.537003

``` ::: ::: ### Categorical summary ::: {#41f2a1aa .cell execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code} categorical_columns = ["Sex"] + (["Diet"] if "Diet" in df.columns else []) for col in categorical_columns: print(f"\nValue counts for: {col}") print(df[col].value_counts(dropna=False)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Value counts for: Sex Sex F 23230 M 20545 O 2 Name: count, dtype: int64 Value counts for: Diet Diet NaN 32008 6 2788 3 2659 4 2516 5 2131 2 1172 7 418 1 34 REGNANC 5 8 5 06 (P.D) 3 N/S 3 07 (P.D) 3 02NEW PA 2 07(P.D) 2 9 2 06 PPREG 1 04NEW PA 1 0595/60 1 6- 1 03(P.D) 1 .... 1 ........ 1 2000 1 1400 1 2200 1 K 1 47 1 35 1 04- 1 6.1 1 06160/100 1 53 1 120/80 1 6(P.D) 1 ------- 1 NEXT TIME 1 58 1 06 (PD) 1 04\ 1 39 1 16 1 Name: count, dtype: int64 ``` ::: ::: ## Data cleaning and outlier handling The goal here is to remove implausible or extreme records that may distort the analysis. ::: {#aaf7a52c .cell execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code} df = df.drop_duplicates().copy() df = df[ ~( (df["BMI"] >= 60) | (df["BMI"] <= 15) | (df["Weight (KG)"] >= 500) | (df["Weight (KG)"] <= 30) | (df["Height (CM)"] > 250) | (df["Height (CM)"] <= 100) | (df["Fasting"] > 50) ) ].copy() ``` ::: ### Quick sanity check after filtering ::: {#6ebe1d76 .cell execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code} df[["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]].describe() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=13} ```{=html}
Age Height (CM) Weight (KG) BMI Fasting
count 43517.000000 10945.000000 11027.000000 43508.000000 43517.000000
mean 47.618561 158.030882 64.392128 25.945947 11.162435
std 12.127001 8.886475 11.665086 4.197449 4.606895
min 3.000000 121.000000 31.000000 15.010751 0.000000
25% 39.000000 151.000000 56.000000 23.138507 7.500000
50% 48.000000 158.000000 64.000000 25.593737 9.800000
75% 55.000000 165.000000 72.000000 28.326580 13.900000
max 110.000000 189.000000 135.000000 57.471264 38.000000
``` ::: ::: ## Feature engineering ### Create age group labels ::: {#c9464559 .cell execution_count=14} ``` {.python .cell-code} df["Age Group"] = np.where(df["Age"] <= 30, "<=30", ">30") df["Age Group"].value_counts() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=14} ``` Age Group >30 39893 <=30 3624 Name: count, dtype: int64 ``` ::: ::: ### Create BMI category ::: {#59ef36e8 .cell execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code} bmi_bins = [0, 18.5, 25, 30, np.inf] bmi_labels = ["Underweight", "Normal", "Overweight", "Obese"] df["BMI Category"] = pd.cut( df["BMI"], bins=bmi_bins, labels=bmi_labels, right=False ) df["BMI Category"].value_counts(dropna=False) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=15} ``` BMI Category Normal 18123 Overweight 17763 Obese 6639 Underweight 983 NaN 9 Name: count, dtype: int64 ``` ::: ::: ## Sample composition ### Participant count by age group ::: {#9583b32f .cell execution_count=16} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.countplot(data=df, x="Age Group", hue="Age Group", palette="Set2", legend=False) ax.set( xlabel="Age Group", ylabel="Count", title="Participant Distribution by Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=16} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Age Group'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Count'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Participant Distribution by Age Group')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-17-output-2.png){width=695 height=456} ::: ::: ### Sex distribution across age groups ::: {#6b982f4d .cell execution_count=17} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.countplot(data=df, x="Age Group", hue="Sex", palette="Set1") ax.set( xlabel="Age Group", ylabel="Count", title="Sex Distribution Across Age Groups" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=17} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Age Group'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Count'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Sex Distribution Across Age Groups')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-18-output-2.png){width=695 height=456} ::: ::: ### BMI category distribution by age group ::: {#74eae590 .cell execution_count=18} ``` {.python .cell-code} g = sns.catplot( data=df, x="BMI Category", hue="Age Group", kind="count", palette="Set2", height=5, aspect=1.6 ) g.set_axis_labels("BMI Category", "Count") g.set_titles("BMI Category Distribution by Age Group") g.ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=15) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-19-output-1.png){width=848 height=488} ::: ::: ## Grouped descriptive analytics ### Summary statistics by age group ::: {#c5922f7e .cell execution_count=19} ``` {.python .cell-code} group_summary = ( df.groupby("Age Group")[["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]] .agg(["count", "mean", "median", "std", "min", "max"]) .round(2) ) group_summary ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=19} ```{=html}
Age Height (CM) ... BMI Fasting

Age Group

>30 39893 49.52 49.0 10.77 31.0 110.0 10049 158.13 158.0 8.91 ... 25.59 4.14 15.01 53.33 39893 11.18 9.9 4.57 0.0 38.0

2 rows × 30 columns

``` ::: ::: ### Summary statistics by age group and sex ::: {#d811ad43 .cell execution_count=20} ``` {.python .cell-code} group_sex_summary = ( df.groupby(["Age Group", "Sex"])[["Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]] .agg(["count", "mean", "median", "std"]) .round(2) ) group_sex_summary ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=20} ```{=html}

``` ::: ::: ## Correlation analysis ### Overall correlation heatmap ::: {#b1492ce9 .cell execution_count=21} ``` {.python .cell-code} corr = df[["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]].corr(numeric_only=True) ax = sns.heatmap( corr, annot=True, cmap="coolwarm", fmt=".2f", linewidths=0.5 ) ax.set_title("Correlation Heatmap of Core Numerical Variables") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=21} ``` Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Correlation Heatmap of Core Numerical Variables') ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-22-output-2.png){width=693 height=435} ::: ::: ### Correlation heatmap for `Age <= 30` ::: {#770a491e .cell execution_count=22} ``` {.python .cell-code} corr_young = ( df.loc[df["Age Group"] == "<=30", ["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]] .corr(numeric_only=True) ) ax = sns.heatmap( corr_young, annot=True, cmap="coolwarm", fmt=".2f", linewidths=0.5 ) ax.set_title("Correlation Heatmap for Age <= 30") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=22} ``` Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Correlation Heatmap for Age <= 30') ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-23-output-2.png){width=693 height=435} ::: ::: ### Correlation heatmap for `Age > 30` ::: {#03f54f3b .cell execution_count=23} ``` {.python .cell-code} corr_older = ( df.loc[df["Age Group"] == ">30", ["Age", "Height (CM)", "Weight (KG)", "BMI", "Fasting"]] .corr(numeric_only=True) ) ax = sns.heatmap( corr_older, annot=True, cmap="coolwarm", fmt=".2f", linewidths=0.5 ) ax.set_title("Correlation Heatmap for Age > 30") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=23} ``` Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Correlation Heatmap for Age > 30') ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-24-output-2.png){width=680 height=435} ::: ::: ## Distribution analysis ### Fasting distribution overall ::: {#bf367ca1 .cell execution_count=24} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.histplot( data=df, x="Fasting", kde=True, bins=30 ) ax.set( xlabel="Fasting", ylabel="Count", title="Overall Distribution of Fasting" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=24} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Fasting'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Count'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Overall Distribution of Fasting')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-25-output-2.png){width=685 height=456} ::: ::: ### Fasting distribution by age group ::: {#0f533943 .cell execution_count=25} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.histplot( data=df, x="Fasting", hue="Age Group", kde=True, bins=30, element="step", stat="count", common_norm=False ) ax.set( xlabel="Fasting", ylabel="Count", title="Fasting Distribution by Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=25} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Fasting'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Count'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Fasting Distribution by Age Group')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-26-output-2.png){width=685 height=456} ::: ::: ### BMI distribution overall ::: {#90c91c2a .cell execution_count=26} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.histplot( data=df, x="BMI", kde=True, bins=30 ) ax.set( xlabel="BMI", ylabel="Count", title="Overall Distribution of BMI" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=26} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'BMI'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Count'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Overall Distribution of BMI')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-27-output-2.png){width=685 height=456} ::: ::: ### BMI distribution by age group ::: {#fcea1ec7 .cell execution_count=27} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.histplot( data=df, x="BMI", hue="Age Group", kde=True, bins=30, element="step", stat="count", common_norm=False ) ax.set( xlabel="BMI", ylabel="Count", title="BMI Distribution by Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=27} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'BMI'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Count'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'BMI Distribution by Age Group')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-28-output-2.png){width=685 height=456} ::: ::: ### Density comparison of fasting by age group ::: {#10bab20d .cell execution_count=28} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.kdeplot( data=df, x="Fasting", hue="Age Group", fill=True, common_norm=False, alpha=0.4 ) ax.set( xlabel="Fasting", ylabel="Density", title="Density of Fasting by Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=28} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Fasting'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Density'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Density of Fasting by Age Group')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-29-output-2.png){width=681 height=456} ::: ::: ## Core relationship analysis ### Age vs fasting #### Scatter plot ::: {#796bc593 .cell execution_count=29} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.scatterplot( data=df, x="Age", y="Fasting", hue="Age Group", palette="Set1", alpha=0.7 ) ax.set( xlabel="Age", ylabel="Fasting", title="Age vs Fasting" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=29} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Age'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Fasting'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Age vs Fasting')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-30-output-2.png){width=667 height=456} ::: ::: #### Mean fasting by age ::: {#eac8aef8 .cell execution_count=30} ``` {.python .cell-code} age_fasting = ( df.groupby(["Age", "Age Group"], as_index=False)["Fasting"] .mean() ) ax = sns.lineplot( data=age_fasting, x="Age", y="Fasting", hue="Age Group", marker="o" ) ax.set( xlabel="Age", ylabel="Mean Fasting", title="Mean Fasting by Age" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=30} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Age'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Mean Fasting'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Mean Fasting by Age')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-31-output-2.png){width=667 height=456} ::: ::: ### BMI vs fasting #### Scatter plot by age group ::: {#470d4bec .cell execution_count=31} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.scatterplot( data=df, x="BMI", y="Fasting", hue="Age Group", alpha=0.7, palette="Set2" ) ax.set( xlabel="BMI", ylabel="Fasting", title="BMI vs Fasting by Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=31} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'BMI'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Fasting'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'BMI vs Fasting by Age Group')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-32-output-2.png){width=667 height=456} ::: ::: #### Regression view ::: {#a8255f90 .cell execution_count=32} ``` {.python .cell-code} g = sns.lmplot( data=df, x="BMI", y="Fasting", hue="Age Group", height=5, aspect=1.4, scatter_kws={"alpha": 0.6} ) g.set_axis_labels("BMI", "Fasting") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-33-output-1.png){width=754 height=464} ::: ::: ### Weight vs fasting ::: {#9b3b99b9 .cell execution_count=33} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.scatterplot( data=df, x="Weight (KG)", y="Fasting", hue="Age Group", alpha=0.7, palette="Set1" ) ax.set( xlabel="Weight (KG)", ylabel="Fasting", title="Weight vs Fasting by Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=33} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Weight (KG)'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Fasting'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Weight vs Fasting by Age Group')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-34-output-2.png){width=680 height=456} ::: ::: ### Height vs fasting ::: {#0fbeb8d3 .cell execution_count=34} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.scatterplot( data=df, x="Height (CM)", y="Fasting", hue="Age Group", alpha=0.7, palette="Set2" ) ax.set( xlabel="Height (CM)", ylabel="Fasting", title="Height vs Fasting by Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=34} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Height (CM)'), Text(0, 0.5, ax.set( xlabel="Sex", ylabel="Fasting", title="Fasting by Sex and Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=35} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Sex'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Fasting'), ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-38-output-2.png){width=667 height=456} ::: ::: ### Age group vs BMI ::: {#e3b0d994 .cell execution_count=38} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.boxplot( data=df, x="Age Group", y="BMI", hue="Age Group", palette="Set1", legend=False ) ax.set( xlabel="Age Group", ylabel="BMI", title="BMI by Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=38} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Age Group'), Text(0, 0.5, 'BMI'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'BMI by Age Group')] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-39-output-2.png){width=667 height=456} ::: ::: ### Violin plot of fasting by age group ::: {#2dd1532f .cell execution_count=39} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.violinplot( data=df, x="Age Group", y="Fasting", hue="Age Group", palette="Pastel1", legend=False ) ax.set( xlabel="Age Group", ylabel="Fasting", title="Fasting Distribution Shape by Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=39} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Age Group'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Fasting'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Fasting Distribution Shape by Age Group')] ``` ::: ::: ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = sns.lineplot( data=df, x="Age", y="Fasting", hue="Sex", style="Age Group", estimator="mean" ) ax.set( xlabel="Age", ylabel="Mean Fasting", title="Mean Fasting by Age, Sex, and Age Group" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=44} ``` [Text(0.5, 0, 'Age'), Text(0, 0.5, 'Mean Fasting'), Text(0.5, 1.0, 'Mean Fasting by Age, Sex, and Age Group')] .cell-output-display} ![](initial_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-48-output-1.png){width=1288 height=1179} ::: ::: ## Group comparison tables ### Mean comparison across age groups ::: {#9b99dab2 .cell
